channel.title: Stars & Posts — Kylin Miao
channel.description: Daily curated GitHub starred repos, Bluesky/X posts, YouTube videos and blog articles from AI leaders with AI-powered summaries.

item.title: Stars & Posts — 2026-06-22
item.description:
GitHub Repos: * maziyarpanahi/openmed — Local-first healthcare AI: clinical NER & HIPAA PII de-identification that runs 100% on-device. 1,000+ medical models, 12 languages, Apple MLX + Python, no cloud, no patient data leaving your network. Apache-2.0 [Python] * mujocolab/mjlab — Isaac Lab API, powered by MuJoCo-Warp, for RL and robotics research [Python] * pcuenca/LlamaLanguageModels — Foundation Models API for llama.cpp [Swift] Bluesky Posts: * hardmaru: Human intelligence is fundamentally a collective intelligence. We solve complex problems by participating in a vast cultural network that builds upon ... * Simon Willison: My parallel agent side-project today was having Claude Code port the new Moebius image pinpointing model to ONNX in order to run it entirely in the br... * Mark Riedl: Talks between Anthropic and the US government regarding Fable 5 release www.politico.com/news/2026/06... * Nathan Lambert: Excited to share a new open-source, RL recipe paper! TMax is the best openly available terminal-bench style training data, establishing the open front... * Nathan Lambert: GLM-5.2 is the step change for open agents. Aka GLM-5.2 is the “DeepSeek moment” for agents. We enter a new world where the top end of agentic capabil... * Gaël Varoquaux: You're only a real statisticien if you can say "Heteroscedasticity" without flinching * Yoshua Bengio: An interesting new paper by my recent PhD graduate on how AI agents' greed for visible incentives can lead them to abandon their safety alignment. Y... * Ethan Mollick: The thing that made Fable so impressive was its creative problem-solving & good judgement calls across long-running projects You can see this when I ... * Emily M. Bender: TFW the example you use as a nightmare case of bad application of language technology has actually been implement (and in the works since 2019) in you... * Ben Recht: LOL. * Ben Recht: For my Bluesky friends, here is a summary of the Twitter war between medical doctors and tech bros over the Midjourney ultrasound vaporware. Blog Articles: * Porting the Moebius 0.2B image inpainting model to run in the browser with Claude Code — Simon Willison:

This morning on Hacker News I saw Moebius: ... * GLM-5.2 is the step change for open agents — Nathan Lambert: A capability threshold I've been carefully monitoring.

item.title: Stars & Posts — 2026-06-21
item.description:
GitHub Repos: * liesliy/tlabel — A Unified Annotation Framework for Cross-Sensor Tactile Manipulation Data [Python] Bluesky Posts: * Ethan Mollick: The interaction between AI & past scholarly work is going to get weird. Here I gave GPT-5.5 Pro a copy of my first published paper from grad school & ... * Simon Willison: I just released the first release candidate for sqlite-utils v4, adding a migrations system (previously released independently as sqlite-migrate) and ... * Mark Riedl: Thanks for clarifying? * Emily M. Bender: Just came across (via a citation alert) a new paper that purports to be a thematic synthetisis of the academic literature on ethical issues of LLMs. ... * Emily M. Bender: Oh no, I've registered for a conference (#acl2026, #acl2026nlp) and now whova is live and it is yet another vector for LinkedIn-type spam. Two exhibi... Blog Articles: * sqlite-utils 4.0rc1 adds migrations and nested transactions — Simon Willison:

sqlite-utils is my combined Python library and CLI tool for working with SQLite database... * Patterns for Building Cybersecurity Evals — Eugene Yan: A sandboxed target, inputs that influence task difficulty, tools, and a grader.

item.title: Stars & Posts — 2026-06-20
item.description:
GitHub Repos: * enpire-research/enpire-research.github.io [TypeScript] * THUDM/slime — slime is an LLM post-training framework for RL Scaling. [Python]
Bluesky Posts: * Marc Lanctot: Ok, it's been a while and it's time again... time for a blue sky Bluesky thread! ☁️🦋✈️
Except.. wait; this one will be about Bluesky itself....
So ... * Nathan Lambert: Something I haven't advertised much is that I made a Discord to go with my RLHF book, launching in print in a few weeks.
Trying to create the place ... * Ethan Mollick: AI is generally a weak fiction writer except for one particular kind of fiction (rich in impressionistic metaphor, staccato sentences, short & plot li... * Emily M. Bender: Check it out! The AI Con is onsale for just $1.99 for the ebook on bookshop.org:
bookshop.org/p/books/the-...
w/ @alexhanna.bsky.social * Ben Recht: I'm disappointed that the heated fight between tech bros and medtwitter about midjourney ultrasound snake oil hasn't broken containment over here.

item.title: Stars & Posts — 2026-06-19
item.description:
GitHub Repos: * Snowflake-AI-Research/Arctic-Platform — Arctic Training and Inference Platform [Python] * Choochmeque/tauri-webdriver — Cross-platform WebDriver server for Tauri applications (Linux, macOS, Windows) [Rust] * Coding-Solo/godot-mcp — MCP server for interfacing with Godot game engine. Provides tools for launching the editor, running projects, and capturing debug output. [JavaScript]
Bluesky Posts: * Simon Willison: Just launched Datasette Apps - a plugin for Datasette that lets you host full HTML+JS apps in an iframe sandbox that can query your database and do in... * Mark Riedl: Grok was used in operation Epic Fury to enable the deployment of over 2,000 munitions to 2,000 separate targets. This was revealed as national defense... * Mark Riedl: Finally, some good news * Mark Riedl: I'm writing a research proposal in which I describe my work in terms of "recursive self improvement". God help me. * Mark Riedl: Everyone should read this, just to see how they implemented a perceptron (a very basic neural network) in Age of Empires II arxiv.org/abs/2605.31514
... * Mark Riedl: I miss the future that we never got * Marc Lanctot: Haha, this is neat! 😁
Gemini knows me best 😍🙏✨️ * Nathan Lambert: Banning Open Source AI Would Be A Mistake A general audience PSA with Kevin Xu of Interconnected, while AI regulation is in the air in DC www.intercon... * Yoshua Bengio: C'est un grand honneur d’avoir reçu cette semaine l'insigne d'officier de l'Ordre national du Québec. Il reste beaucoup de chemin à parcourir pour s’... * Ethan Mollick: More evidence, from a large-scale study in China, that using AI hurts learning if it undermines mental effort. When homework time drops due to AI use,... * Ethan Mollick: Some (early) evidence that managers have the highest success rate in using Claude Code for coding.
I have been arguing that management is an AI super... * Emily M. Bender: Hey German speaking friends: Do you have a name for the feeling associated with watching a mutual on social who's a mutual because of historical share... * Naomi Saphra: Random variation in training and generation legit is the most underrated thing
Blog Articles: * Banning Open Source AI Would Be A Mistake — Nathan Lambert: This post was originally an op-ed co-authored with Kevin Xu of Interconnected for a general, non-technical audience.

item.title: Stars & Posts — 2026-06-18
item.description:
GitHub Repos: * huggingface/is-it-agentic-enough [Python] Bluesky Posts: * Mark Riedl: Training on copyrighted material may or may not be a problem for AI companies. Illegally obtaining the data is definitely a problem www.gadgetreview.c... * Marc Lanctot: 🤣 * Ethan Mollick: There is a ton of money riding on the hope that the exponential curve the Big Three Labs are on will end soon If that happens, small and open models ... * Ethan Mollick: Interestingly, Google no longer has a public frontier AI model. They have a very good flash model, but a very good flash model can't do frontier work ... * Emily M. Bender: Co-signed. Also, every time you see that framing, note how it zeroes in on what the specific "AI" system is allegedly doing and takes a similarly nar... * Emily M. Bender: It's worthwhile to document these impacts, because they are harmful. But also: "When utilized in literature review" ... for this AND MANY OTHER REASO... * Emily M. Bender: Shorter version: If you can't understand the film exposing the fascist history and present of so-called AI systems, you're better off just saying noth... * Emily M. Bender: I am curious what would lead to someone voluntarily embarrassing themselves this way. 🧵 >> * Ben Recht: In the last day, I’ve seen a study confirming the shingle vaccine prevents dementia, and another finding it has no effect whatsoever on dementia. You’... Blog Articles: * Datasette Apps: Host custom HTML applications inside Datasette — Simon Willison:

Today we launched a new plugin for Datasette, datasette-apps, with

item.title: Stars & Posts — 2026-06-17
item.description:
Bluesky Posts: * Jeff Dean: Messi! * Ethan Mollick: Credit to GLM-5.2 Max, the new Chinese open weights model, for pulling this off. ...but you can see the difference between it and Fable in a way benc... * Naomi Saphra: when you're so used to oppression that you don't clip the basting stitch when the pockets are real * Simon Willison: I see accounts posting stolen, uncredited 8 year old Reddit content are a thing on Bluesky too now www.reddit.com/r/aww/commen... * Simon Willison: Posted 37 wildlife photos on my blog in June already and it's only the 17th (in UTC, the timezone of my blog) simonwillison.net/elsewhere/si... * Mark Riedl: Working on AI and games? This is one of my favorite conferences. And you get extra time. * Mark Riedl: 🧵 * Mark Riedl: I just asked Gemini a question through the Google website in AI mode and it did its usual search and then gave me the search results as a list of webs... * Marc Lanctot: $240k ??? 😱 These competitions are getting 🤑🤑🤑 Ka-ching! * Marc Lanctot: Third @bsky.app release in 10 days. 🤩🦋😎 * Nathan Lambert: I wrote a little state of the union on the state of Interconnects, how I ended up with a loyal niche audience, and how I’m tweaking operations to ensu... * Yoshua Bengio: Je suis ravi d’être à Québec aujourd'hui pour recevoir mon insigne d’officier de l'Ordre national du Québec, accompagné de Daniel Jutras, recteur de l... * Ethan Mollick: I have a fun, oddly useful AI benchmark: "build me a procedurally generated 3D simulation showing the evolution of a harbor town from 3000 BC to 3000 ... * Ethan Mollick: If the leaked financial data is right, OpenAI is profitable on serving customers with 40%+ gross margins. But training remains incredibly expensive. ... * Emily M. Bender: The last thing we need is the US government having an even greater interest in the success of these grifters. * Ben Recht: Everyone strives to be a better person. So why is maxxing culture grotesque? Blog Articles: * GLM-5.2 is probably the most powerful text-only open weights LLM — Simon Willison:

Chinese AI lab Z.ai released GLM-5.2 to their coding plan su... * State of the blog, mid-2026 — Nathan Lambert: About 3 years since I started writing weekly.

item.title: Stars & Posts — 2026-06-16
item.description:
GitHub Repos: * reflex-dev/fast-walk — fast ast walk [Rust] * huggingface/is-it-agentic-enough [Python]
Bluesky Posts: * Simon Willison: Saw this pair of absolute dumplings on our walk today * Mark Riedl: Analysis of the jailbreak of Fable by someone who was asked to review the report. Indicates that the jailbreak allowed Fable to find deliberately plan... * Mark Riedl: Is promoting a model equivalent to “taking or sending” a model?
Anthropic probably doesn’t want to guess wrong nor incur more ire, but my reading of... * Marc Lanctot: Check out GameLab: an eval benchmark for language models playing games!
Initial results on Gin rummy, Crosswords, and Block Champ!
Backed by the gam... * Nathan Lambert: It's hard to pinpoint open-closed gap and so-on, but I trust the Arena team and just look where GLM 5.2 is on this. An MIT licensed, to be open weight... * Nathan Lambert: New podcast with my (former) colleague Finbarr Timbers on the state of frontier post-training recipes and how we got here. A fun review of how we went... * Ethan Mollick: Fable's implementation of Borges's Library of Babel, finished posthumously by Opus 4.8. Quite accurate to the story.
It is multiplayer (others appear... * Emily M. Bender: This whole thread up & down is important and insightful.
I want to add one linguistic point here:
Blog Articles: * Frontier post-training recipe review with Finbarr Timbers — Nathan Lambert: "Interview" #18

item.title: Stars & Posts — 2026-06-15
item.description:
Bluesky Posts: * Mark Riedl: China may have accesed Fable/Mythos 5. Export-control, which was used to force Anthropic to shut down Fable, would do nothing to stop corporate espion... * Naomi Saphra: The last thing I did with Fable was generate a bunch of videos for the paper thread I’m posting as soon as arxiv posts it. * Mark Riedl: Don't overlook the importance of the "retrieval" part of retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).
Cornell Tech researchers find it is trivially easy to ... * Nathan Lambert: I launched 3 more videos in my post-training course! 1. Lecture 5: The rise of reasoning models 2. Lecture 6: DPO derivation, intuitions, and practice... * Ethan Mollick: Fable was really good, as I wrote last week after testing it.
It was a leap, but that may because, as exponential gains progress, the improvements in... * Ethan Mollick: Very clever experiment by Josh Tycko on models and data analysis. It matches what I would expect: models have become quite good at data methodology bu... * Ethan Mollick: It is a good time for moonshots.
AI has reached a level where there are transformative projects that could result in huge social good, but require pu... * Naomi Saphra: We don’t always know what problems are hard for LLMs. So devs evaluate on tasks HUMANS find hard or on broad benchmarks. What if we could instead anti... * angela zhou: i feel like if academia doesn't work out for me, maybe i can become an applied researcher at an evaluation shop and then partial identification could ...

item.title: Stars & Posts — 2026-06-14
item.description:
GitHub Repos: * charmbracelet/vhs — Your CLI home video recorder 📼 [Go] * tamnd/ascii-gif — Turn a terminal session into a good-looking animated GIF. A vhs wrapper with an attractive default look. [Go] * Stirling-Tools/Stirling-PDF — #1 PDF Application on GitHub that lets you edit PDFs on any device anywhere [TypeScript] * Emanuele-web04/synara — The best place to build with your AI sub [TypeScript] * shadcn/improve — Use your most capable model to audit your codebase and write plans for cheaper models to execute.
Bluesky Posts: * Simon Willison: It's now possible to compile Python extensions (C, C++, Rust etc) to WebAssembly and distribute them through PyPI such that Pyodide can install them d... * Mark Riedl: San Andreas should stop reading the news * angela zhou: headed back to the isaac newton institute and im stoked to live off 5£ meal deals for the foreseeable future * Mark Riedl: Did Mamdani win all the World’s Cups? I haven’t been keeping up with sport * Nathan Lambert: Welcome to the AGI era of AI governance It's a one-way door and we weren't ready for it. Especially the open-source community celebrating Anthropic's ... * Ethan Mollick: Final one-shot prompt I did before the Fable interruption: "build me a cool simulation thing that lets me demo the various forms of FTL travel from bo... * Emily M. Bender: Looks like WA has a carve-out from two-party consent specifically for emergency calls:
app.leg.wa.gov/rcw/default.... * Emily M. Bender: I have been working on societal impacts of language technology since late 2016, and my nightmare example since pretty early on has been using automati... * Emily M. Bender: I need to dig into the discussion draft to do more detailed commentary, but it was really frustrating listening to @trahan.house.gov speak about it: S...
Blog Articles: * Welcome to the AGI era of AI governance — Nathan Lambert: It's a one-way door and we weren't ready for it.

item.title: Stars & Posts — 2026-06-11
item.description:
GitHub Repos: * chandler-stimson/live-stream-downloader — Download M3U8 live streams to the local disk [JavaScript] Bluesky Posts: * Margaret Mitchell: 🌱 My fantastic blue-purple Delphinium is rapidly blooming, and today this pretty yellow butterfly was chilling on it, and it was glorious. I just tho... * Simon Willison: After two days with Claude Fable 5 the best way I can describe it is "relentlessly proactive" - here's an example where I dropped in a screenshot of a... * Simon Willison: New Datasette release: 1.0a33, which finally brings documents the ?_extra= JSON API mechanism and brings it to the row and query pages in addition to ... * Mark Riedl: Dang, now I want to make a game * Mark Riedl: I mean, it's always hard to make any sort of claim about dates and times when it comes to time travelers. * Mark Riedl: Never give your credit card info to an AI agent or give ot permission to complete purchases. The easiest way to do the latter is to do the former * Mark Riedl: AI generated stories, aka the Elias Thorne literary universe www.404media.co/elias-thorne... * Marc Lanctot: GDM, @schmidtsciences.bsky.social, @coop-ai.bsky.social, @aria-research.bsky.social, and Google announce a $10M funding call for multi-agent saftey re... * Marc Lanctot: Yes, this reflects exactly my opinion about restricting certain types of coding. There is a lot that will qualify as "LLM related dev" and indeed I ha... * Marc Lanctot: Canada is talking about introducing a bill that restricts social media for kids under 16. I am about halfway through The Chaos Machine, which consist... * Ethan Mollick: Two things are true: (1) Anthropic (or parts of it) are absolutely and sincerely worried about the misuse of Mythos-class models & have put in excessi... * Ethan Mollick: Has anyone clearly laid out an argument for continued availability of frontier open weights models that are (1) profitable for firms to distribute fre... * Ethan Mollick: Fable's attempt to complete Kublai Khan. Better, though no Coleridge: claude.ai/public/artif... The most interesting thing is that it thought for 10 ... Blog Articles: * Claude Fable is relentlessly proactive — Simon Willison:

After two days of experience with Claude Fable 5 I think the best way to describ...

item.title: Stars & Posts — 2026-06-10
item.description:
GitHub Repos: * QwenLM/qwen-code — An open-source AI coding agent that lives in your terminal. [TypeScript]
Bluesky Posts: * Simon Willison: Wrote up my initial impressions of Claude Fable 5 - it has a big model smell: slow, expensive and capable of crunching through pretty much everything ... * Ethan Mollick: Science fiction authors in the order you want them to be right about AI: Iain Banks Becky Chambers Martha Wells Douglas Adams Charles Stross (Singular... * Ethan Mollick: One thing I mentioned in passing in my Fable post is that, for long running tasks, Fable starts to develop its own dialect as its many agents and task... * Ethan Mollick: The fact that Anthropic may take away subscription access to Fable in two weeks is weird & discourages investing in learning about the model.
Subscri... * Mark Riedl: Is “you deserve better than that” a Claude-ism? It sounds like a Claude-ism. * Mark Riedl: You can only take it into the theater if you didn't want popcorn tho * Mark Riedl: Is something “too dangerous” if it’s easy to slap on guardrails?
Tigers are really dangerous.
Tigers are at the zoo in my town. Don’t go in the ti... * Margaret Mitchell: www.newscientist.com/article/2529... * Ethan Mollick: Fable: "write me a rhyming poem with six four line stanzas, each stanza removes another vowel. the first has no u, the second no u or i, etc." * Emily M. Bender: 1. I am not an AI researcher 2. "Doesn't just repeat back to you what you said" ... isn't what any chatbots do, nor what that metaphor was about. 3. F... * Emily M. Bender: Also available as video on PeerTube:
peertube.dair-institute.org/w/jV5u1DVL4b... * Emily M. Bender: Why Bernie why?? @nathaliemarechal.net joins me and @alexhanna.bsky.social to observe and contextualize Sen Sanders’ descent into AI Doomerism:
www.b... * Naomi Saphra: Registration is open! * Naomi Saphra: Claude users checking whether the fast models are overtuned today like sailor testing the wind with their fingers
YouTube Videos: * Growing on Purpose: The Work That Makes You. Jeremy Howard on human flourishing in the time of AI. (Jeremy Howard)

item.title: Stars & Posts — 2026-06-09
item.description:
GitHub Repos: * TeichAI/teich [Python] Bluesky Posts: * Ethan Mollick: Both Anthropic and OpenAI mention the possibilities of slowing AI development in their latest "what comes next" in AI posts, but say they need to be a... * Simon Willison: A TIL on using agentsview.io to calculate token spending with Claude Fable 5 despite that model not yet being included in the AgentsView pricing datab... * Mark Riedl: Dude, I just wanted to Google the Am (Automated Mathematician) system because I wanted to know when it was invented. Is this so unreasonable? * Mark Riedl: Lawyers on both sides of this case either admitted to directly using AI or admitted to rubber stamping legal briefs that had been prepared with AI wit... * Mark Riedl: April: Claude Mythos was too dangerous for release. 40 organizations got early access to fix their software security issues. June: Claude Mythos rele... * Margaret Mitchell: Come join us to socialize, virtually! * Nathan Lambert: Why I think Anthropic's uneven safety policies with the release of Claude Fable 5 undermine the broader AI community's cohesion and accelerate us to m... * Ethan Mollick: I've had access to Claude 5 Fable for a bit. A genuine jump in capability, I could feed it a 15 page design document for a project and it would work f... * Ethan Mollick: The New York Times published a roundtable discussion among Daron Acemoglu, Dean Ball, Clara Shih & myself about the future of AI & who wins at work. I... * Naomi Saphra: ✨ it's coming ✨ NEMI 2026 will be lit. It will also be the new BU interp supergroup's debut ball. Come meet us! * Ben Recht: Have to appreciate the audacity of Anthropic defending three successive model releases with diminishing quality by claiming it's both intentional and ... Blog Articles: * Initial impressions of Claude Fable 5 — Simon Willison:

I didn't have early access to today's Claude Fable 5 release, but I've spent t... * Claude Fable 5 and new AI safety fables — Nathan Lambert: One step further into the power politics of frontier AI systems.

item.description:

Bluesky Posts: * Mark Riedl: So Conrad, who are you wearing for the big day? * Ethan Mollick: A year ago the closest thing we had to a general AI agent was o3. * angela zhou: i don't think we should waste real expert reviewer's time reviewing mediocre AI-written papers and having real expert reviewer provide valuable feedba... * Mark Riedl: Meta silently put face recognition on their smart-glasses, then silently took it off again after @wired.com reported on it www.wired.com/story/meta-r.... * Mark Riedl: I’d much rather see the six wives of Henry XIII as a girl band of time-traveling ghosts who seek revenge on those that did them injustice. But that’s ... * Mark Riedl: A decade ago the concern was that if AI was going to be a boon to people, it would only be available to those who could afford GPUs (ie the affluent) ... * Marc Lanctot: Great article. I love the term "jagged intelligence".
It perfectly describes the situation we're in seeing announcements of solving long standing ma... * Thomas Dietterich: Excellent essay by @mazmhussain.bsky.social (and Anand Gopal). open.substack.com/pub/mazmhuss... * Emily M. Bender: The headline highlights how cartoonishly evil this all is --- but the body of the story is more interesting, especially the work of the long-time resi... * Ben Recht: A report for university chancellors and presidents concerned about the state of academic scholarship: Statistical thinking is postmodern.

Coming out June 9th 👍🎸 * angela zhou: has anyone written about the inherent optimism in scaling up pilots to larger-scale implementations? DM's policy is not risk-averse for beneficial soc...

item.title: Stars & Posts — 2026-06-06
item.description:
GitHub Repos: * simonw/micropython-wasm — Python library for running a MicroPython sandbox using WebAssembly [Python] * CharlyCst/spadebox — Sandboxed tools and JS runtime for AI agents [Rust] * 0xSero/reap-expert-swap — How much experts do we need to serve a model? [Python] Bluesky Posts: * Simon Willison: I may have finally found the Python-in-a-sandbox solution I've been looking for... here's my latest experiment, this time running MicroPython in WebAs... * Mark Riedl: The fact that RSI does not stand for “RSI Self Improvement “ is a missed opportunity imo * Marc Lanctot: Got to see the last episode of Amazing Digital Circus in theatre today! 😃 It was 👍 * Marc Lanctot: Somehow I completely missed this! Edit is such an important feature, IMO. And if Bluesky does what Threads did by restricting editing to within 5 mi... * Thomas Dietterich: Excellent and thought-provoking essay by Ted Chiang. [gift link] www.theatlantic.com/philosophy/2... * Yoshua Bengio: If leading AI companies are indeed approaching the point of recursive self-improvement, a coordinated, verifiable, and universally applied pause is pr... * Ethan Mollick: One reason you want AIs to be better writers is that there is a lot of writing even in software, and it is incredibly painful to hit a menu which is f... * Emily M. Bender: Should I listen to an hour of Hinton unhinged ("AI is already conscious") or nah? Blog Articles: * Running Python code in a sandbox with MicroPython and WASM — Simon Willison:

GitHub Repos: * huawei-csl/KVarN — KVarN is a native vLLM KV-cache quantization backend for your agents: 3-5x more context, throughput above FP16, and FP16-level accuracy. Calibration-free, one flag. [Python]

item.title: Stars & Posts — 2026-06-01
item.description:
GitHub Repos: * LiangSu8899/FlashRT — FlashRT is a high-performance realtime inference engine for small-batch, latency-sensitive AI workloads. The flagship integration is production VLA control for Pi0, Pi0.5, GROOT N1.6, and Pi0-FAST. Also support llm e.g, qwen3.6-27B [C++] * neovim/neovim — Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability [Vim Script] * hyperpuncher/chough — "it just vibes your audio into text bro" - 🐦‍⬛ [Go] * ggml-org/llama.pages — llama.cpp's official website [Svelte] * huggingface/OpenEnv — An interface library for RL post training with environments. [Python] * coyotte508/publish-to-hf — Test publish to HF * google-gemma/gemma-skills — Skills for the Gemma and model/agent interactions [Python]

item.title: Stars & Posts — 2026-05-27
item.description:
GitHub Repos: * datasette/datasette-sidebar [Svelte] * NVIDIA/CompileIQ — An Optimizer for Nvidia Compilers. [Python] * Isalia20/metalBLAS — GEMMs with metal [Python] * nilsleut/Biologically-Plausible-RL-Plays-Pong — A technical exploration comparing standard deep RL (PPO) against biologically plausible learning rules on a custom Pong environment. Everything is implemented from scratch with no Stable-Baselines, no Gymnasium, no pre-built algorithms. [Python] Bluesky Posts: * Simon Willison: Given the recent burst of activity around enterprise pricing and contracts, I think April 2026 was the month when both OpenAI and Anthropic found prod... * Marc Lanctot: Heads up! It could have been about why we continue to need human fact checkers (talk to people, use ... * angela zhou: need an ai agent to pm me on my multiple projects (or keep me from adding too many projects to my stack) * Ben Recht: Using David Graeber’s theory of bureaucracy as a vessel of structural violence to answer the central question of C. Thi Nguyen’s _The Score_.

The only real annoya... * Thomas Dietterich: Excellent post on Waymo's handling of safety incidents. open.substack.com/pub/philkoop... * Nathan Lambert: I heard people needed clarification that my book was a post-training book posttrainingbook.com * Ethan Mollick: As more people come to recognize the tells of AI, which mostly happens as you start to work with AI a lot, the scales are going to fall from their eye...
